services.name: Pothole on Road
services.introText:
Use to report a pothole on City Streets (not in alleys) so pothole can be patched and immediate hazard addressed.

DO NOT USE: To report potholes in alleys or on bridges, report sinkholes or issues with manhole covers. Use Repair Roads to request major pavement or cobblestone repairs. See alternate request links below.

Emergencies: Please call 804-646-5100 for emergencies that pose an immediate danger to persons or property. 

Location: Please make sure location provided is accurate so inspector can locate the issue. Intersections may be provided by using the “&” or “@” sign.  (For example: E Broad St & N 2nd St).

Already Reported?: Check the pins that appear on the map around the location entered to see if pothole has already been reported.

Time to Repair: Potholes will be repaired with asphalt patch within 14 days, weather permitting. See What Happens Next section for more detail.

Privacy Notice:  This is a PUBLIC service request. Your personal information will NOT be displayed but details entered in the Description field will be publicly visible to all users.  Please do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: Report Neighborhood Roadway Safety Concern
services.introText:
For residential neighborhood concerns on roadways only. This request is to report safety concerns on residential neighborhood streets related to pedestrian, bicycle, or motorized vehicle roadway safety. 

NOTE: For expressways and major primary roads contact VDOT at 1-800-367-7623

Do not use for obstructions or hazards on other public rights of way (e.g. sidewalks, alleys, medians,  etc.) 

For Public Right of Way hazards or obstructions please use the Report Public Right of Way Concerns request.

For speeding concerns please use our Report Speed Violations in Community request.

For road repairs, potholes, curbs and ramps or pavement marking concerns, please visit 311's Roads, Alleys, Sidewalks and Ramps category and choose the request best suited to your concern.

For traffic signs or signal repairs, please visit the Lights, Signs and Signals category and choose the request best suited to your concern.

Location: Please make sure location provided is accurate. For Intersections use “&” or “@” sign.  (EX: E Broad St & N 2nd St). 

Time to Respond: Please allow 90 Days for Transportation Engineering team to respond. See What Happens Next section for more detail.

DID YOU KNOW? Speed data for your street is available on the Citywide Speed Data map. Data for crashes, injuries, fatalities and more on the Vision Zero Dashboard. DPW Transportation Engineering team proactively reviews this data to inform their many transportation improvement programs.

Privacy Notice:  This is a PUBLIC service request. Personal Contact information is NOT displayed but Description field is visible to all users. Do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: Handicap Parking Sign
services.introText:
When to Use:  Use this to request a designated disabled parking zone in a residential area, including placement of a Handicap Parking Only sign.  Or to request repair or removal of an existing Handicap Parking sign. All requests related to Handicap zones and signs require an on-site inspection.

Fee:  There is no fee for handicap parking applications or signs.

For New Signs:   A completed, signed application and all additional documentation requested in the application must be submitted for review and approval.  Application form and guidelines available at Handicap Parking Guidelines and Application Form. Signed, completed applications can be submitted online, by mail or in person. 

ONLINE: Completed and signed application and copies of supporting documentation can be attached to this RVA311 request.

By Mail:  Completed and signed application and copies of supporting documentation can be mailed to address indicated on the application form.

In Person:  Completed and signed application can be delivered in person to:

900 E. Broad Street, Room 102.

What to Expect:  Once your application is received, the City will review it along with the documentation provided within 1-3 business days of receipt and additional steps will be coordinated. What Happens Next section can provide more details. 

For Existing Signs:  The request to remove or repair will be reviewed within 1-3 business days of receipt.

services.name: Repair Existing Streetlight
services.introText:
For an EMERGENCY:  such as downed or broken light poles, poles hit by a vehicle; hanging wires or cables, etc. please call  804-646-8426 so emergency crews can assess damage and address any immediate danger.

Do Not Use for Gas Streetlights: All calls regarding gas lamps should be directed to DPU Emergency Services at 804-646-4646 (option 1).

When to Use:  Use to report NON-EMERGENCY issues with an existing street light, such as an outage, light blinking, or damage to the fixture. Your contact information is required so the team can clarify location details if necessary.

Location: PLEASE PROVIDE AN ACCURATE ADDRESS OR INTERSECTION. Streetlights are often close together so accurate location details help crews find the correct light. Providing the Pole Number if visible is the best way to make sure crews can identify the exact light in question - even if servicing in daylight when outage or blinking is not visible. 

BE AWARE:  Not all streetlights are maintained by the City of Richmond. Some are the property of and maintained by Dominion Power. The City will assess and transfer the request to Dominion where appropriate but time to repair will depend on Dominion schedule and resources.

What to Expect:

General Outages: Please allow 45-65 Days for inspection, assessment and repairs of general issues such as light out or blinking, damaged light fixture, etc.

Minor Infrastructure Damage Assessment: Damaged or broken pole, down or damaged cables or wires, etc. should be reported as emergencies to 804-646-8426. Once any initial danger is managed please allow 90-180 days for inspection and assessment of any underlying infrastructure issues.

Major Infrastructure Repair: Repair of Infrastructure related damage such as damaged poles, cables or wires may take up to 12–24 months.

Please Note: This is a PUBLIC service request. Although your personal information will not be displayed, details entered in the Description field will be publicly visible to all users. Please do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: Request New Tree Planting
services.introText:
When to Use:  Use to request a New Tree Planting.  Requests received after August 31 cannot be considered until the following year's planting season.

What to Expect:

City arborists must inspect the site to confirm it is available and plantable.

  • Arborist has final say and will approve species as selected or recommend appropriate species for each site.
  • Planting is scheduled during the dormant season - Winter.
  • Planting is dependent on available resources. 
  • The City is unable to accommodate citizens' specific schedule requests.

Please Note: It is City policy to plant the largest appropriate species for each available planting site. Large species will not be approved for undersized sites, nor will small species be approved for planting in large sites.

Sites may be deemed unsuitable for a variety of reasons, including environmental concerns, site is in an area slated for redevelopment, or site has already been adopted for planting through the City's Adopt a Tree Program.

If you would like more information on the Adopt a Tree Program click the links below.

Adopt a Tree Program Description

Adopt a Tree Application

Residents may also request a special permit to perform maintenance on a City tree or remove a City tree altogether at their own expense. For more information, please visit the Urban Forestry page on RVA.gov

services.name: Assess Existing Tree or Stump
services.introText:
Please Read...

If this is an Emergency : For tree related emergencies such as 1) Trees down on utility lines 2) Trees blocking street, alley or sidewalk please call 804-646-5100.

When to Use: Use this request to:

* Report a non-emergency issue with a City tree that requires inspection and assessment

* Request removal of stumps left behind 6 months or longer after tree was removed.

Do Not Use for:

* Emergencies - Please use number provided above.

* Trees in City Alleys are the responsibility of the private property owner and are NOT maintained by the City of Richmond unless a downed tree is blocking the alley.

* Trees on private property are the responsibility of the private property owner, even if they extend into City spaces. 

* Non-city trees. Not all trees in the City are maintained by the City. To confirm whether the tree in question is a City tree check the latest Tree Inventory Map

* Roots Damaging Sidewalks should be submitted as a Repair Sidewalk or Ramp request even when damage is caused by tree roots.

What to Expect:

* A City arborist will attempt to inspect the tree within 5 business days of a request being received (weather and resources permitting) 

* Arborist will evaluate the tree to assess what action, if any, is needed.

* Work will be prioritized based on the arborist's findings and planned based on safety, urgency and available resources

* Note that in some cases, the arborist may determine no safety issue exists and NO IMMEDIATE ACTION is needed. 

Please be patient. If arborist recommends removal of a tree, this can be a lengthy process, sometimes taking 12 months.

Stump Removal:  Stump removal is part of a tree removal job, however, it is completed at a later time to allow Ms. Utility to confirm presence of underground utility lines in the vicinity. 

Please only submit a separate stump removal request if the stump has been left longer than 6 months after the tree was removed.

Residents may also request a special permit to perform maintenance on a City tree or remove a City tree altogether at their own expense. For more information, please visit the Urban Forestry page on RVA.gov

Duplicate Requests: Your concern may already be reported. Requests deemed to be duplicates may be closed (before the work has been completed) and the concern addressed using the previously submitted request. Duplicate requests create extra administrative process and can be a hindrance to teams in addressing concerns, so please avoid duplicate submissions when possible.

Your Privacy: The personal contact information provided in this request will never be displayed. However, this is a PUBLIC request so any details entered in the Description field will be publicly visible to all users. Please do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: Report Issue on Richmond Park Trail
services.introText:
If this is an Emergency:  Call 9-1-1 and provide the nearest trail marker ID for medical or safety emergencies while on the trail.

FOR TRAILS ISSUES ONLY: Use this Request to report a non-emergency issue on hiking trails within a Richmond City Park. (e.g. downed trees or limbs, damaged bridge or boardwalk, missing signs or markers, etc.)  See our OTHER Parks Issues, request for non-trail concerns.

PLEASE INCLUDE PHOTOS whenever possible. Photos help inform trails team as to urgency and location of issue as well as what tools or equipment that may be needed when inspecting.

Do Not Use for Emergencies:  3-1-1 is for non-emergency issues only and response is not immediate. Call 9-1-1 for any emergency.

Do not Use for NON TRAIL issues: For other general parks related concerns email askparkrec@rva.gov

What to Expect: 

  • A trails team member will attempt to inspect issues reported within 7 days to assess what action is needed (weather and resources permitting) 
  • Needed work will be prioritized based on safety, urgency and available resources
  • Team will attempt to complete necessary work within 30 days (NOTE: Inspection may reveal NO ACTION is needed)

Duplicate Requests:  Richmond trails are heavily used so your concern may already be reported. When reporting an issue, review the Open Requests Nearby to avoid submitting a duplicate request.

YOUR PRIVACY:   The personal contact information provided in this request will never be displayed.  However, this is a PUBLIC request so details entered in the Description will be publicly visible to all users. Do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: How to Get Free Bulk Trash Pick Up
services.introText:
FREE Bulk Items Pick Up - Every Two Weeks

The Department of Public Works' Bulk Items crew will attempt to pick up your bulk items every other week on the same week as your recycling. No call or special scheduling required.

Who is Responsible: City of Richmond - Department of Public Works. The Bulk collection happens on your recycling week, but our Recycling Vendor CVWMA is NOT responsible for picking up bulk items. The Bulk Collection program is managed directly by the City.

What to Expect: Note that bulk pickup is dependent on available resources; therefore it may sometimes take up to two recycling cycles for items to be collected by our crews.

  • On your recycling week, place bulk items out by your trash on your scheduled collection day in the same location as household trash 
  • Bulk Collection will typically occur on your collection day. 
  • If not collected on your collection day, the crew will attempt to return and collect by Saturday of your recycling week, depending on available resources. 
  • If not collected by the Saturday of your second scheduled recycling week, the crew should collect on your next scheduled recycling week.
  • If items are not collected by your second scheduled recycling week after being placed out for pickup, please contact 3-1-1
  • Do not contact CVWMA–this is NOT a recycling program.

Prohibited items:

  • Construction debris
  • Tires
  • Carpet
  • Auto parts
  • Appliances (a Paid Services request is needed for appliance pickup) 

Did You Know? SAME DAY pick up can be requested for a fee. Use the Paid Services Request Page to request a paid pick up.

Visit the City's Bulk and Brush Pickup for more details.

services.name: Report Litter on Public Property
services.introText:
For Issues on Public Property Only

When to Use:   Use this request to report EXCESSIVE ACCUMULATIONS of litter or debris, on public property, including bike lanes. This applies to junk, demolition materials, upholstered furniture or excessive amounts of trash, garbage or leaves that have been piled in a specific place on the property.  A few bottles, leaves or stray pieces of trash is not considered an excess accumulation.   

Location: Note that public spaces do not always have a distinct address. If necessary use the nearest valid address and provide more specific location details in the description field.  Intersections may be entered by using the “&” or “@” sign.  (For example: E Broad St & N 2nd St).

Who is Responsible:  Please note that YOU may be responsible.

If the excess accumulation is 1) on someone's Private Property or 2) on public property, alleys or rights-of-way that join or are otherwise against someone's private property, the private property owner is responsible for removal of the accumulation.   This includes excess garbage, mattresses, couches, chairs or other types of indoor furniture left on public property - REGARDLESS OF HOW IT GOT THERE.  To report issues related to private property you may submit an Unlawful Accumulation of Debris request to Code Enforcement.

The City will collect any excess accumulations that are clearly on free-standing public property, such as parks, schools, medians on multi-lane roads, expressway roadsides, etc.

What to Expect:  Once requests are received and the City confirms the accumulation is clearly on public property, the City will schedule cleanup as soon as resources are available.  This will typically be within 3-5 business days.   If the City determines the accumulation is on or adjoined to private property, they will transfer the request to Code Enforcement for investigation and the private property owner may be cited and fined.

DO NOT USE this request to report a missed trash collection, including trash over flow in parks or other public space.  To report missed trash collection, please see Report Missed Trash Collection

Your Privacy: The personal contact information provided in this request will never be displayed. However, this is a PUBLIC request so any details entered in the Description field will be publicly visible to all users. Please do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: Storm Cleanup for Branches and Tree Debris
services.introText:
FOR STORM RELATED EMERGENCIES such as downed power lines, blocked roadways, alleys or sidewalks, please call non-emergency police at 

804-646-5100.  

Assistance with non-emergency removal of limbs, branches and other tree debris related to a storm event is handled as part of the City's bi-weekly Bulk and Brush pickup program.  YOU DO NOT NEED TO CALL OR SUBMIT A REQUEST FOR PICKUP.

What to Expect:  After major storm events City crews are usually working 24/7.  First priority is given to emergency situations that pose a threat to citizens' health and safety.  Once all emergencies have been addressed, extra trucks and crews will help clear remaining debris on behalf of citizens. 

Please be aware: City crews do not enter private property even if a City tree is involved.  It is the responsibility of the property owner to place any debris at the property line where it is accessible to City crews.

To have storm debris collected:  

  • Remove any fallen tree, limbs or branches from structures, driveways or yard
  • Cut tree debris into sizes that can be handled by the Bulk and Brush crews
    • Bulk and Brush crews do not have chain saws or other cutting equipment
    • Pieces can be no larger than 48" (4 feet) in order to be picked up
  • Place the debris at your property line
  • Debris will be collected as soon as crews are available

Please be patient.  Depending on the severity of the damage, the need may be high.   Crews will work through the City's neighborhoods as quickly as possible, beginning with those our inspectors report have sustained the most damage.

services.name: Report Graffiti
services.introText:
What is Graffiti:  Graffiti is the unauthorized application of any writing, painting, drawing, etching, scratching or marking of an inscription, word, figure, or design of any type on any public or private building.

When to Use:  Use this request to report graffiti on public or private property. 

Who is Responsible: 

Graffiti on PRIVATE Property:

The City retains the right to remove graffiti on private property that is visible from public property such as roads or alleys, and is deemed offensive, vulgar or promotes gang-related activities.  However, generally it is the responsibility of the property owner to remove graffiti on their property.   

If you are the property owner, check with your insurance provider to see if graffiti is covered by your policy as an act of vandalism.  

If you are not the property owner, report the incident to the landlord or property owner. 

Additional Requirement:  The city cannot take any action to remove graffiti on private property without the written consent of the property owner.  Use this link to obtain the needed Consent To Enter and Release of Liability Agreement form.  This form must be completed and signed by the property owner and returned to the address below.

City of Richmond 
Department of Public Works 
900 E. Broad St., Room 704 
Richmond, VA 23219

Graffiti on PUBLIC Property:

The City will investigate reports of graffiti on public property but removal is based upon available resources.  

What to Expect:

While graffiti can be an unsightly nuisance and a blight on our communities, it rarely poses a public safety threat.  Therefore, City resources to remove it are reserved for the most extreme cases such as those involving hate speech, language that is vulgar or promotes gang activity.

Graffiti on PRIVATE Property:

  • The City will send a letter to the property owner requesting the graffiti be removed.
  • If the property owner does not comply, the City is authorized to remove graffiti. 
  • If the City exercises its authority to remove, the City is held harmless of any damage to the structure or surrounding error that may occur as part of the removal.
  • In some cases, the cost of removal may be charged back to the property owner.

Graffiti on PUBLIC Property

  • The City will inspect and document any graffiti reported on publicly owned structures (e.g. public sidewalk, building, bridge abutment, etc.). 
  • However, the City may determine that removal is not feasible depending on safety risks, cost and available resources.  

Please Note:  Sometimes removal on historical buildings is not permitted due to risk of irreparable damage to the structure. 

HELP PREVENT GRAFFITI:  If you know of individuals or groups who are defacing property in your neighborhood, (click here) or call non-emergency police at 804.646-5100 to report and request an investigation.

Please be a specific as possible regarding the location.

Your Privacy: The personal contact information provided in this request will never be displayed. However, this is a PUBLIC request so any details entered in the Description field will be publicly visible to all users. Please do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

•  Paint being poured into or near the storm drainage system
•  Changing oil or antifreeze over or near a storm structure
•  Washing motor pool vehicles where the runoff could drain into the storm drainage system
•  Washing dumpster pads and allowing the runoff to drain into the storm drainage system
•  Discharging swimming pool water directly into the storm drain system without de-chlorinating the water
•  Discharging swimming pool water backwash (containing bacteria, sediment, chlorine, etc.)

Restrictions: Please note the following restrictions:

What to Expect:

  • Your request will be closed to be transferred to the Zoning and Code Enforcement system and this 311 Request will be marked as Completed

  • If a violation is found the property owner will be issued a Notice of Violation. (NOV)

  • If corrective action is not taken the city may contract work to be completed.
  • If the city corrects the violations, the cost of the work will be billed to the property owner.

services.name: Report Public Right of Way Concern
services.introText:
Please Read. 

There are many types of right of way concerns. To get your concern to right team please review below to make sure this is the correct form.

For open grates or coal chutes in the sidewalk that present an immediate danger please call 804-646-5100. If just deteriorated and in need of repair you may submit this form.

For fallen trees or limbs blocking street, alley or sidewalk please call 804-646-5100.

See Abandoned Vehicle on City Streets or On Street Parking Violation to report these concerns.

Use Maintain Vegetation on Public Property for tree branches or vegetation obstructing street, alley or sidewalk.

Use Report Private Property Construction without a Permit for private property construction without proper permits.

Use THIS form to report

  1. Construction Related Blockage or Hazard in the public Right of Way, including failure to provide pedestrian walkway;
  2. Garbage Cans frequently left blocking the Right of Way
  3. Restaurants that have tables or seating blocking the public Right of Way
  4. Signs placed in the public Right of Way
  5. Damaged sidewalk grate or coal chute that needs repair BUT DOES NOT pose an immediate danger

Please be aware:  The City does issue permits that allow for temporary obstructions to the public right of way.  For large scale construction permits can be for long periods of time.  Search the City's Online Permit Portal to see if the address has an active permit. 

For more information on the City's Right of Way Management team visit the City's Right of Way Management page

PLEASE NOTE:  This is a PUBLIC service request.  Although your personal information will not be displayed, details entered in the Description field will be publicly visible to all users.  Please do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: Report Private Property Construction without a Permit
services.introText:
Use For:  PRIVATE PROPERTY CONCERNS ONLY

When to Use:  Use to request investigation of active construction that does not appear to have proper permits, including building or demolition, plumbing, electrical, gas, fire alarms /sprinklers, or other mechanical work. This applies to commercial or residential PRIVATE property.  

Do not Use: For public right of way concerns (e.g. obstructions or hazards on roads, alleys or sidewalks). Please use the Report Public Right of Way Violation request for those concerns.

When Submitting your Request:  Please be sure to indicate the exact location of construction so that proper research into whether a permit exists for the construction can take place.  

Please be Aware:    

  • Many smaller projects such as sheds, fences, decks, pools, etc., may not require a permit if they meet the exemption requirements.  Details about exemptions can be found here on the City of Richmond Website.
  • Construction Permits must be displayed in a window or otherwise near the front entrance.
  • Construction Permits display the date issued but do not display an expiration date because they may be renewed multiple times (provided appropriate fees paid and inspections performed.)
  • You may be able to determine whether a permit exists by searching the City's Online Permit Portal for the address where the construction is occurring.

What to Expect:  Once your request is received the Permit team will begin investigation within 2 business days. Please allow 15 business days to complete investigation and close your request.

  • The City will research the address reported to confirm whether an active permit is on file.
  • If YES
    • Your request will be updated to indicate the reported work is covered by permit.
    • If needed, the City will remind the permit holder to make sure permits are displayed in a visible location on the site.
  • If there is NO active permit, the City will issue a Stop Work order until the proper permits are obtained.  

Your Privacy: The personal contact information provided in this request will never be displayed. However, this is a PUBLIC request so any details entered in the Description field will be publicly visible to all users. Please do not include personal information about yourself or others in this field.

services.name: Parking Citation Concerns
services.introText:
When to Use:  Use to request assistance with issues related to a parking citation/ticket such as DMV holds, delinquent notice received in error, or suspending late fee accrual while you contest a ticket in court. To appeal a citation received, Citizens have two options: Administrative Review or Contest in Court.

Appeal Citation by Administrative Review:  Visit the Administrative Review section under Tickets on the City's main Parking page for more details and to submit an official request for an Administrative appeal. This type of appeal is only for citations received due to issues such as broken meter, disabled vehicle, medical emergencies, stolen license plates or stolen vehicle, missing signage, or other similar issue. 

Inform us you will Contest your Parking Ticket in Court:  To Contest Parking tickets a court date must be scheduled in Richmond Traffic Court and you or a representative must appear in court. Submit this request to inform us you will Contest the citation in court and stop accrual of late fees and delinquent notices while you await your court date.  Late fees do not accrue while you await your court date. However, the court may hold you responsible for paying any court fees even if the judge rules in your favor.  If the judge rules against you, you will be responsible for paying the original Citation amount as well as any court fees as instructed by the court.

Request Removal of DMV Hold for Unpaid Ticket: If you paid your parking ticket but it is not showing as properly credited, DMV may place a hold and prevent you from renewing licenses or registrations.  You may submit this request to have your payment verified and the DMV hold removed.

Delinquent Parking Citation Notice:  If you paid your parking ticket or it was excused but you have received a letter indicating your ticket is past due and additional late penalties are being applied, submit this request so we may investigate and correct the situation.

DO NOT USE for TOWING:  If your vehicle has been towed from a City right of way or City owned parking, contact Siebert's Towing at 804-233-5757.

What to Expect:  Parking violations are managed by the Department of Public Works, Parking Division.  Our team makes every effort to respond to all requests within five (5) business days.

To verify the correct sector and appropriate contact information, visit the Richmond Police Patrol Services page
